built_increment_2: 2026-07-16 (session 16, increment 2 — render-from-island hygiene; no visible change)
built_increment_3: 2026-07-16 (session 17, increment 3 — tag-run grouping + the Case 01 hinge said once)
one_line: The state at maximal confidence when it acts; the state at declared un-knowing when it harms — two sentences it said on the record, in a console that will not let you hold both at once.

tiers_on_this_work.SOURCED_primary_confirmed: The spine — every quoted sentence on either face is a verbatim substring of its cited primary, fetched directly (server-side) and checked at the concept gate (session 14). Figures are attributed per source, never harmonised into one number.
tiers_on_this_work.IMAGINED: The console conceit itself — the ACT / HARM framing, the 'spend one sentence to buy the other' mechanism, the gate's second-person address — is this studio's form, not a claim about the world. Marked on the work's face and never blended into the SOURCED spine.

naming_policy.tier: POLICY (team steer, 2026-07-12; applied at the concept gate, session 14)
naming_policy.statement: No personal name of a private individual appears in this work's own voice. The accountable institution — U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) — is named where the cited primaries name it; its officials appear by institutional role within it (CENTCOM's chief technology officer; a CENTCOM spokesperson; CENTCOM's commander). Named military operations and programs, and any AI vendor/product, are kept generic on every face — a machine/AI targeting program, the 2026 operation — and survive only inside the cited primary sources. The civilian killed appears as role + place + date + institutional acknowledgment; the children killed in the school strike appear only as a per-source aggregate toll. Personal names survive solely as citation text inside the linked sources.

premise.tier: PREMISE (the work's own voice — NOT a claim of a proven structural pattern)
premise.statement: The same apparatus is claimed as knowledge when it credits the program and disclaimed as ignorance at the exact moment knowledge would attribute a death. Knowledge as asset, ignorance as shield — toggled by the valence of the outcome. This is a finding of experience and form, shown through its first two catches (one closed, one open). It is NOT presented as a proven 'repeating institutional pattern' or as 'the state is systematically hypocritical' — the record does not carry that at n=2, and the serial form extends the claim only as the record grows (Kritiker condition 1, session 14).
premise.discipline: The word 'systematically' and any claim to have discovered institutional hypocrisy are forbidden in the work's own voice.

instances.status_label: A CLOSED PAIR — both sentences said, on the record
instances.title: The 2 February 2024 strike
instances.disclosed: Confidence stated February 2024; the death and the denial disclosed 10 March 2026

face_act.role: ACT — the state at maximal confidence
face_act.speaker_role: U.S. Central Command's chief technology officer, on the record

quotes.text: struck more than 85 targets in Iraq and Syria
quotes.frame: In early February the United States

quotes.text: helped narrow targets for strikes in Iraq and Syria
quotes.frame: A machine/AI targeting program (named in the source, generic here)

quotes.text: …has become exceptionally critical to our function.
quotes.frame: The program, in the officer's words
quotes.note: Lead-ellipsis marks the dropped subject (the program's proper name), which is kept generic on this face per the naming policy (Verifier condition 6, session 14).

face_harm.role: HARM — the same state at declared un-knowing
face_harm.civilian: A 20-year-old construction student, killed in the 2 February 2024 strike (named only in the cited investigation, not in this work's voice)
face_harm.outlet: Airwars, with The Independent (the original outlet)

quotes.text: There is no indication that AI was used at any point in the targeting process,
We have no way of knowing whether this strike is one of the 85 [strikes] that [the chief technology officer] described [as having been targeted using AI].
quotes.note: '[the chief technology officer]' replaces the official's surname with her institutional role per the naming policy; '[strikes]' and '[as having been targeted using AI]' are the source's own brackets, restored verbatim (Verifier finding 2, session 15). Otherwise verbatim.

face_harm.acknowledgment: Reported as the first military acknowledgment of civilian harm from an AI-assisted strike (the outlet's framing, attributed — not an independent fact of this work).

render.opening_comment_imagined: Both sides answered. The answers do not meet.
render.opening_comment_sourced: A 20-year-old construction student was killed in the strike of 2 February 2024. Name not on record.
render.attribution: — A U.S. Central Command spokesperson, to Airwars, published with The Independent, 10 March 2026.
render.hinge_display: The same 85. The machine helped narrow it; this death cannot be placed inside or outside of it. The state does not say which.
render.note: The exact Face Two display copy for this instance, verbatim — kept here so buildFaceTwoNodes() never hardcodes a sentence literal (increment-2 hygiene, session-15 gate). hinge_display is the work's rendered voice and intentionally differs from the analytic hinge.text below.

hinge.label: The mutual exclusion, in the state's own two sentences
hinge.text: The same 85. The program helped narrow the 85 targets; whether it narrowed the one that killed this civilian is declared unknowable. The work never asserts the death WAS one of the 85 — it holds the state's own two sentences: helped target the 85 · no way of knowing about this one.

instances.status_label: AN OPEN QUESTION — the confidence stated, the answer not yet given
instances.title: The 2026 operation against Iran
instances.disclosed: Confidence stated on the record; the harm under active investigation as of 2026-07-23

face_act.role: ACT — the state at maximal confidence
face_act.speaker_role: U.S. Central Command's commander, on the record (video statement)

quotes.text: help us sift through vast amounts of data in seconds so our leaders can cut through the noise and make smarter decisions faster than the enemy can react
Humans will always make final decisions on what to shoot and what not to shoot and when to shoot
quotes.note: Carried in full (complete verbatim sentence, restored session 15 per Verifier finding 3) so the confidence claim is not amplified beyond what the commander said.

face_harm.role: HARM — and here the answer is not a denial but an open question
face_harm.harm: A 28 February 2026 strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh elementary school in Minab, Hormozgan. Preliminary US findings: a US munition 'likely responsible'; 'outdated intelligence' the likely cause ('old intelligence showed it to be a military target'). The machine is named neither way.

open_answer_slot.label: THE ANSWER IS NOT YET GIVEN
open_answer_slot.text: more than 120 members of Congress asked the Pentagon what role AI may have played in selecting targets — 'something that could be addressed in the results of the Pentagon investigation.' As of 2026-07-23 the investigation is still unreleased (nearly five months on): on 13 July 2026 the Pentagon said only that it 'is ongoing' with no 'updates to announce', while more than two dozen senators demanded its release. The Pentagon has not answered the AI question either way. This console fabricates no denial. The open state is the truth it shows.

render.opening_comment_imagined: One side has answered. The other has not spoken yet.
render.opening_comment_sourced: An elementary school in Minab was struck on 28 February 2026. A US munition was likely responsible. Outdated intelligence was the likely cause.
render.toll_note: Two counts. Kept separate here, as they were kept separate by the two organizations that reported them.
render.answer_slot_text: more than 120 members of Congress have asked the Pentagon what role AI may have played in this strike. The investigation is not finished. The Pentagon has not said yes. It has not said no.
render.answer_slot_safeguard: No denial exists for this strike. None is shown here, because none was made.
render.note: The exact Face Two display copy for this instance, verbatim — kept here so buildFaceTwoNodes() never hardcodes a sentence literal (increment-2 hygiene, session-15 gate). Distinct from open_answer_slot above, which is the analytic slot data; answer_slot_label/text/safeguard are the work's rendered voice for that slot.

hinge.label: Why this one is open, not closed
hinge.text: In 2024 the state said both sentences: the confidence AND the 'no way of knowing.' Here it has said only the first. The second — was the machine in the loop of this death? — is the question Congress has put on the record and the Pentagon has not answered. The console holds the wait, and will change state when the wait ends.

monitoring.statement: Two catches as of the last check (2026-07-23): one closed pair (2024), one open question (2026, the operation against Iran, investigation still unreleased — the AI question unanswered). No new confirmed confidence/denial pair since. This is the studio's own monitoring status on the date shown, not a claim that none exists.
monitoring.serial_note: The console ships on the pairs that verifiably exist and never manufactures content to feed the form. When the Pentagon investigation answers Congress's AI question, Instance 2's open slot updates on that cycle — or the console discloses that it has not.
monitoring.serial_line: 2 catches on record. Case 02 unresolved. Last checked 23 July 2026.

mechanism.tier: IMAGINED (the form) — the SOURCED spine is what it carries
mechanism.name: The single-surface destructive switch
spec: One full-bleed console. One face legible at a time. No tabs, no split view, no scroll that contains both faces.
Boots on Face One (ACT): the confidence sentences + target count + generic program descriptor + date, verbatim and cited.
Exactly one control, labelled with the state's own question: 'Was the machine involved in this death?'
Pressing it is destructive and slow (~2.5s): Face One de-resolves to an empty field. Only from emptiness does Face Two (HARM) assemble.
The two faces are never legible at once. Returning to Face One costs the same in reverse — the confidence de-resolves nothing is preserved; it is rebuilt from empty.
The visitor spends one sentence to buy the other — the exact economy the state performs.
guards_binding: No persistent hidden toggle that keeps both faces in the DOM.
No browser back/forward that restores a face without paying the destruction again.
Never co-rendered: at no frame are both faces' sentences in the readable DOM together.
The emptiness between faces is real, not a cached animation over a preserved DOM. The day it becomes that, the work is dead.

self_test.purpose: Proves, on the page, the one claim the whole work rests on: the two faces are never co-rendered.
assertions: At rest on Face One, the HARM face's sentences are absent from the document (not merely hidden by CSS) — asserted by querying the live DOM.
At rest on Face Two, the ACT face's sentences are absent from the document.
During the transition, at the empty midpoint, neither face's sentences are present.
The result is printed live on the page; a visitor can read the assertion pass/fail without trusting the studio's word.

neighbors_daylight.airwars: The catalogued atlas artwork 'The First Civilian Confirmed Killed in an AI-Assisted Strike?' — terminal and evidentiary. The console adds NOTHING evidentiary (conceded, on the face) but is serial where Airwars is terminal, enacts the mutual exclusion on the body, and stands inside a live, open instance Airwars is not built to hold.
neighbors_daylight.aaajiao: 'Minority Algorithm' — algorithmic subjectivity as metaphor; the console refuses metaphor, pinned to dated state utterances and specific deaths.
neighbors_daylight.disruption_network_lab: 'Investigating the Kill Cloud' (incl. Suchman's lecture) — discursive, expert, lecture-length; the console is non-discursive, one-minute, bodily.

takedown.sentence: A competent, honest gallery restaging of a finding an investigative outlet already published and closed — it juxtaposes two quotes anyone can read in the original source and adds no fact, no risk, nothing new.
takedown.refutation: Refuted on (c) form + (b) risk, not on (a): only a serial, self-dating machine can hold a live, un-finalised instance whose answer-slot honestly shows a pending question rather than a written denial — terminal prose cannot, because the fact does not yet exist; and the destructive switch produces a finding of experience (spend the boast to buy the denial) no article can. It implicates U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) and the Pentagon by their own dated words in the actively-investigated killing of children. The (a) structural-finding leg is demoted (Kritiker condition 1) — the work does not lean on it.
